commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh At the noise of the stamping of the hoofs of his strong horses, at the rushing of his chariots, and at the rumbling of his wheels, the fathers shall not look back to their children because of feebleness of hands; Marching. Literally, "pomp "(Haydock) or warlike apparatus. (Haydock) Protestants, "stamping of the hoofs of his strong horses, at "(Haydock) Children. Fear makes one forget the nearest relations, while each one provides for himself, chap. , Micheas vii. , and Matthew x.
